blockingIssues.description: The trybuild suite does not include a compile-pass case proving nested sensitive derives work when plaintext child types intentionally omit non-essential traits like PartialEq/Eq/Display, so VAL-NS-008 remains unproven.

The reviewed tests only cover top-level wrong-context and override/default isolation; there is no nested secure leaf regression proving sibling-context failure for field-account overrides, so VAL-CROSS-002 remains unproven.

There is no nested Store regression that saves a parent with multiple nested secure leaves using distinct field-account overrides and then proves wrong-leaf decryption fails, leaving the feature contract uncovered in persisted paths.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance for verification-only reviews of already-landed features so workers know when they must prove the exact contract with focused evidence instead of treating nearby pre-existing tests as sufficient.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The metadata/bounds and override-isolation reviews both found workers completing features on HEAD using surrounding tests that did not directly prove the promised contract.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the concrete SensitiveShape abstraction in shared knowledge.
rejectedObservations.reason: Useful but belongs in .factory/library, and updating docs was not necessary to complete this scrutiny synthesis.

rejectedObservations.observation: Clarify GitNexus fallback availability in worker guidance.
rejectedObservations.reason: The repo already documents the fallback in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md; the review did not establish a concrete project-state change needed inside this milestone synthesis.

rejectedObservations.observation: Record that no dedicated nested override isolation regression exists yet in .factory/library/sensitive-crypto.md.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is accurate but should be addressed by the fix feature that adds the missing regression rather than by the scrutiny synthesizer.
